title: Lance of Longinus and Angel DNA
display_title: Lance of Longinus and Angel DNA
wikitext:
[]: Lance of Longinus and Angel DNA             Quote Post by Guy » Wed Mar 30, 2011 5:39 pm I was watching episode 22 and thinking about why the lance of longinus was so necessary in an impact and came up with a theory i at least haven't heard before. During second impact there was human DNA on the lance that went into Adam. So that can be read as a offspring of Lilith DNA put in Adam. For the human side third impact to happen perhaps Seele was planning on having an angels DNA on the lance by killing one with it. Perhaps using it on one of the later angels to stunt Liliths growth for as much time as possible. Gendo's plan didn't necessarily involve the lance since it seems like they were fine having it in the moons orbit. Tell me what you think?

[]: Re: Lance of Longinus and Angel DNA             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 30, 2011 6:45 pm Guy wrote: During second impact there was human DNA on the lance that went into Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy » Wed Mar 30, 2011 7:12 pm I think i always put those two together since the response to DNA diving is trying to get the lance out. For Lilith having the lance inside her seems more like a countermeasure to third impact. So I found it weird they will try to take it out of Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 30, 2011 9:42 pm Guy wrote: I think i always put those two together since the response to DNA diving is trying to get the lance out.

[]:             Quote Post by Sik » Wed Mar 30, 2011 11:10 pm Just have to ask since im sure Reichu know's a lot about that "deleted escene" I saw it on youtube but in spanish but I trust even more on jap-to-eng-translation. Man A "It's still where it was brought ashore after arriving from the Dead Sea last week."

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 30, 2011 11:44 pm The conversation between Gendo and Keel happened on August 15, nearly a month before the rest of the footage. Sik wrote: If they found Adam and the Lance together, what was the Lance doing at the Dead Sea? they took it to Seele's Base? (dont remember where I read Seele has some sort of base there), and they were bringing it back to the Dogma in Antarctica? Is there a EGF thread where that scene is better explained? x_x

[]:             Quote Post by Sik » Thu Mar 31, 2011 12:26 am Seele had the Spear sent to some other research facility at the Dead Sea, where they modified it in secret. (What if Adam had gone out of control during this time? Tough luck, I guess?)

[]:             Quote Post by Guy » Thu Mar 31, 2011 12:37 am So do we know why Gendo didn't need the lance while SEELE did? I guess he wasn't gonna go THAT far in his impact?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Mar 31, 2011 1:19 pm Gendo just wanted to go inside Unit 01 to be with Yui. He didn't necessarily plan to alter the whole of the world like SEELE did.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 31, 2011 1:28 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Gendo just wanted to go inside Unit 01 to be with Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Mar 31, 2011 4:39 pm That 'sinks in' comment... sounds a bit like what happened to 01 in EoE, doesn't it? Perhaps a Seed doesn't get all SToL-ed when that happens, just evas do that.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Mar 31, 2011 5:21 pm (a) Adam's S2 engine tp go out of control and make her self-destruct... (b) ...and paradoxically revert to embryonic form at the same time.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 31, 2011 7:34 pm Monk Ed wrote: You keep saying "paradox paradox". (snip)
summary: []: Lance of Longinus and Angel DNA             Quote Post by Guy » Wed Mar 30, 2011 5:39 pm I was watching episode 22 and thinking about why the lance of longinus was so necessary in an impact and came up with a theory i at least haven't heard before. During second impact there was human DNA on the lance that went into Adam. So that can be read as a offspring of Lilith DNA put in Adam. For the human side third impact to happen perhaps
